content: Hey, I'm back from my sabbatical and trying to get back into a routine. I spent the last 3 months traveling through Eastern Europe, and it was incredible.
Welcome back! It sounds like you had an amazing experience. What was your favorite part of the trip?
Definitely the food in Budapest, I fell in love with goulash. And the people, everyone was so friendly. I even got to try my hand at beekeeping in a small village in Romania.
That sounds like a unique experience. Beekeeping is one of your hobbies, right? How did you get into it?
Yeah, I've been keeping bees for about 5 years now. I started when I was working as a civil rights attorney in New York City, it was a great way to unwind after a long day of work.
It's great that you have a hobby that helps you relax. Do you find that your experience as an attorney influences your approach to beekeeping?

Actually, yes. I've found that my attention to detail and organizational skills have really helped me with managing my beehives. And, I've even started a small beekeeping club in my community, we meet every Thursday at 6 PM.
That's fantastic. It's great that you're sharing your passion with others. Have you thought about how you'll balance your beekeeping hobby with your work as an attorney now that you're back?

Well, I'm planning to start with small steps, maybe take on a few cases and see how it goes. I've also been thinking about performing stand-up comedy again, I used to do it in college and really enjoyed it.
That's a great idea. It's always good to have a creative outlet. Do you have any upcoming gigs or events planned?
Not yet, but I'm thinking about signing up for an open mic night at a local comedy club. Maybe I'll even try to incorporate some beekeeping jokes into my routine.
I'm sure that would be a hit. If you don't mind me asking, how do you plan to prioritize your time between work, beekeeping, and comedy?

I've been thinking about that a lot, and I've decided to wake up at 5:30 AM every day to get a head start. I'll use the morning to respond to emails and plan out my day, then head to the office or the beehives depending on my schedule.

That sounds like a solid plan. It's great that you're being intentional about your time management. Have you considered how you'll handle the physical demands of rock climbing, another one of your hobbies, after being away for 3 months?

Yeah, I'm planning to start with some easier routes and gradually work my way back up to more challenging climbs. I've got a trip planned to Yosemite with some friends in October, so I want to make sure I'm in shape for that.
That sounds like an exciting adventure. Are you planning on taking any time off from work to prepare or will you be fitting your training into your daily routine?
I'll probably take a few days off before the trip to get in some extra training and make sure my gear is in good condition. My boss is really understanding, so I'm sure it won't be a problem.
It's great that you have a supportive work environment. Do you have any other big plans or goals for the next few months?

Actually, yes. I'm thinking about running a 10K in November, so I'll need to start training for that soon. And, I've been considering volunteering at a local community garden to help with their beekeeping efforts.
That's a great idea. Giving back to the community is always rewarding. We can discuss more about your training plan and volunteering opportunities if you'd like.
That would be great, thanks for the offer. I think that's all for now, I've got a lot to think about and plan out.
You're welcome, it was great chatting with you. Feel free to reach out if you need any more advice or just want to talk about your progress.
